assumptions: The recognised dividend receivable is a foreign-currency-denominated intragroup monetary item.
The related exchange differences affect consolidated profit or loss because they are not fully eliminated on consolidation.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 allows a recognised asset or liability to be a hedged item, so the foreign currency risk of a qualifying intragroup monetary receivable can be designated in a fair value hedge.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 also permits a recognised asset or liability to be designated for cash flow variability attributable to a particular risk, making the foreign currency exposure on a qualifying intragroup monetary receivable a genuine cash flow hedge route.
